F0001.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M, state tokens, compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, and optional value-token registries used by prior metric and dimensionality
F0001.label: DEPENDENCY

F0002.body: A function-like assignment is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation F(x,v) where x is a carrier token or state token and v is a token in an explicitly declared finite value-token r
F0002.label: FUNCTION_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0003.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assume F(x,v) exists for all x, nor uniqueness of v for any x.
F0003.label: FUNCTION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0004.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert that F induces an axis, coordinate system, basis, parameterization, or ordering of the carrier.
F0004.label: NONAXIS_GUARANTEE

F0005.body: No admissible function-like assignment may include any universal schema asserting that carrier tokens are exhaustively represented or enumerated via F-values.
F0005.label: NO_GLOBAL_PARAMETERIZATION_SCHEMA

F0006.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert that shared function values imply identity, equality, or indistinguishability of tokens.
F0006.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_FUNCTION_VALUES

F0007.body: No admissible function-like assignment may introduce any schema permitting replacement of one token by another on the basis of F.
F0007.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_FUNCTION_VALUES

F0008.body: Any assertion of F(x,v) that is used jointly with transport must not include any schema forcing transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to F-
F0008.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBILITY

F0009.body: Any assertion of F(x,v) that is used jointly with obstruction must not include any schema forcing obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to F-values.
F0009.label: OBSTRUCTION_COMPATIBILITY

F0010.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert existence of a total function over all carrier tokens or all state tokens.
F0010.label: NO_TOTAL_FUNCTION_BY_DEFAULT

F0011.body: No admissible function-like assignment may introduce any order relation on V and then assert a universal induced order on carrier or state tokens from F.
F0011.label: FORBIDDEN_ORDER_SCHEMA

F0012.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert any universal additivity, linearity, or homomorphism-like schema for combining F-values across transport composition or path concatenation.
F0012.label: FORBIDDEN_ADDITIVITY_SCHEMA

F0013.body: Admissibility of promoting certain families of function-like assignments into true axes or coordinate systems is open, provided this introduces no substitution power and remains removable.
F0013.label: AXIS_EXTENSIONS

F0014.body: Admissibility of using function values in optimization-like selection is open, provided time, probability, and utility semantics are not introduced as primitives.
F0014.label: OPTIMIZATION_EXTENSIONS

F0015.body: Admissibility of introducing order on value tokens is open, provided it does not induce global ordering or parameterization of the carrier by default.
F0015.label: ORDERED_VALUE_EXTENSIONS

F0016.body: This specification presupposes frozen axis-like function admissibility for individual function relations F_i(x,v), and frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.
F0016.label: DEPENDENCY

F0017.body: A finite axis-set is admissible only as an explicitly declared token Σ together with a finite declared membership relation Mem(Σ,F) where each member F is an admitted axis-like function relation symbo
F0017.label: AXIS_SET_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0018.body: No admissible axis-set Σ may contain any symbol that is not an admitted axis-like function relation symbol.
F0018.label: AXIS_SET_SCOPE_RESTRICTION

F0019.body: No admissible axis-set may assert that Σ determines a coordinate system, frame, basis, or global parameterization of carrier tokens or state tokens.
F0019.label: NONCOORDINATE_GUARANTEE

F0020.body: No admissible axis-set may assert any rank, completeness, spanning, or exhaustiveness property of Σ relative to the carrier or any family of relations.
F0020.label: NO_RANK_OR_COMPLETENESS

F0021.body: No admissible axis-set may assume linear independence, orthogonality, functional independence, or non-redundancy among its members.
F0021.label: NO_INDEPENDENCE_ASSUMPTION

F0022.body: No admissible axis-set may assert closure of Σ under any combination rule, nor assert that Σ generates or spans any larger class of functions.
F0022.label: NO_CLOSURE_OR_SPAN

F0023.body: No admissible axis-set may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to joint use of members of Σ.
F0023.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0024.body: No admissible axis-set may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to joint use of members of Σ.
F0024.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0025.body: No admissible axis-set may assume all its member functions are defined for all carrier tokens or all state tokens.
F0025.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0026.body: No admissible axis-set may assume existence of a single axis-set Σ that applies globally to all compatibility components or all declared admissible domains.
F0026.label: NO_GLOBAL_AXIS_SET

F0027.body: No admissible axis-set may introduce any schema that induces a global order on carrier tokens or state tokens by aggregating values across members of Σ.
F0027.label: FORBIDDEN_INDUCED_ORDER

F0028.body: No admissible axis-set may assert that tuples of values across members of Σ form vectors, coordinates, or elements of any structured product space with substitution power.
F0028.label: FORBIDDEN_VECTOR_SCHEMA

F0029.body: Admissibility of promoting certain axis-sets into coordinate frames or bases is open, provided this remains removable and introduces no substitution power.
F0029.label: COORDINATE_FRAME_EXTENSIONS

F0030.body: Admissibility of orthogonality-like or independence-like relations among members of Σ is open, provided no metric, rank, or completeness primitives are introduced.
F0030.label: ORTHOGONALITY_EXTENSIONS

F0031.body: Admissibility of completeness or spanning-like notions for Σ is open, provided no global parameterization or coordinate semantics is asserted by default.
F0031.label: COMPLETENESS_EXTENSIONS

F0032.body: All named frozen admissibility specifications are authoritative and are not modified by any candidate proposal document.
F0032.label: FROZEN_STACK_AUTHORITY

F0033.body: A candidate proposal is a finite plain-text document consisting only of (i) a candidate header token, (ii) declarations, (iii) witness declarations, (iv) compatibility claims, (v) failure conditions,
F0033.label: CANDIDATE_DOCUMENT_FORM

F0034.body: The first non-empty line of a candidate proposal document MUST be a single fixed token string naming the candidate; this token string is the candidate’s identity and must be treated as uninterpreted.
F0034.label: CANDIDATE_HEADER_TOKEN

F0035.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT rename, paraphrase, alias, or reinterpret its own header token, and MUST NOT rename, paraphrase, alias, or reinterpret any frozen specification name or frozen symbol name
F0035.label: NO_RENAMING_RULE

F0036.body: Any versioning of a candidate identity MUST be encoded only in the candidate header token string; no semantic meaning of version increments is admitted at the kernel level.
F0036.label: VERSIONING_AS_TOKEN_ONLY

F0037.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a declaration block that enumerates exactly the set of new symbols it proposes and exactly the set of frozen symbols it uses.
F0037.label: DECLARATION_BLOCK_REQUIRED

F0038.body: New symbol declarations MUST be limited to (i) finite token registries, (ii) relation symbols over previously admitted token types, and (iii) classifier symbols over previously admitted token types; n
F0038.label: NEW_SYMBOL_DISCIPLINE

F0039.body: A candidate proposal MUST list all frozen symbols it invokes; omission of an invoked frozen symbol from the usage list is a failure condition.
F0039.label: FROZEN_SYMBOL_USAGE_LIST

F0040.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a witness block whenever it makes any claim about non-commutation, obstruction, nontriviality, order-sensitivity, non-flattenability, incompatibility, or any other cl
F0040.label: WITNESS_BLOCK_REQUIRED

F0041.body: A witness is admissible only as a finite syntactic binding between declared symbols/tokens; witnesses MUST NOT be treated as semantic, evidentiary, evaluative, probabilistic, temporal, or causal objec
F0041.label: WITNESS_SYNTAX_ONLY

F0042.body: Each witness declaration MUST specify the witness token name and the finite tuple of declared tokens/symbols it binds; witness typing MUST be explicit and must not assume identity or equality.
F0042.label: WITNESS_TYPING

F0043.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a compatibility claim block that lists which frozen specifications it claims to satisfy.
F0043.label: COMPATIBILITY_CLAIM_BLOCK_REQUIRED

F0044.body: Each compatibility claim MUST be stated as a finite declarative statement referencing only declared symbols and/or listed witnesses; each claim MUST be written so that its well-formedness can be check
F0044.label: CLAIM_FORM

F0045.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT assert that any structure is admissible “by default,” “implicitly,” or “because it is standard”; admissibility must be claimed only via explicit compatibility claims agai
F0045.label: NO_IMPLIED_ADMISSIBILITY

F0046.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain an explicit failure condition block enumerating falsifiers as syntactic conditions (e.g., undefined symbol use, forbidden symbol introduction, missing witness where r
F0046.label: FAILURE_CONDITION_BLOCK_REQUIRED

F0047.body: A candidate proposal MUST declare that if any listed failure condition holds, the candidate is eliminated as a candidate; no patching, repair, reinterpretation, or partial acceptance is permitted with
F0047.label: NO_PATCHING_RULE

F0048.body: Candidate elimination MUST NOT modify, weaken, override, or retroactively reinterpret any frozen admissibility layer; candidate failure affects only the candidate’s status, not the frozen stack.
F0048.label: NON_BACKFLOW_GUARANTEE

F0049.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT introduce as admitted primitives any time-like, probability-like, causality-like, optimization-like, ontological, or teleological structure; if referenced, such items mus
F0049.label: FORBIDDEN_PRIMITIVE_IMPORTS

F0050.body: A candidate proposal MAY include an OPEN-only list of candidate-family header tokens as non-binding placeholders; listing a family does not assert existence, preference, correctness, or admissibility.
F0050.label: CANDIDATE_FAMILY_LISTING

F0051.body: Additional format constraints (e.g., canonical ordering of blocks, stricter typing grammars, stronger witness schemas) are OPEN provided they do not introduce new admitted primitives and do not alter
F0051.label: FUTURE_FORMAT_REFINEMENTS

F0052.body: This specification presupposes admitted finite axis-sets Σ with membership Mem(Σ,F), admitted axis-like functions, and frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.
F0052.label: DEPENDENCY

F0053.body: A completeness- or spanning-like claim is admissible only as an explicitly declared unary relation symbol Comp(Σ) ranging over admitted axis-set tokens Σ.
F0053.label: COMPLETENESS_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0054.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assume Comp(Σ) holds for all admitted axis-sets, nor that Comp(Σ) is decidable or witnessed for any Σ by default.
F0054.label: COMPLETENESS_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0055.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert or imply that any Σ satisfying Comp forms a basis, coordinate frame, or coordinate system for carrier or state tokens.
F0055.label: NONBASIS_GUARANTEE

F0056.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert or imply rank, dimensionality, minimal cardinality, or uniqueness of any Σ satisfying Comp.
F0056.label: NORANK_NODIMENSION_GUARANTEE

F0057.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert that Comp(Σ) implies identity, equality, or substitutability of any carrier tokens, state tokens, or function symbols.
F0057.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_COMPLETENESS

F0058.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or function symbol on the basis of Comp.
F0058.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_COMPLETENESS

F0059.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Comp declarations.
F0059.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0060.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Comp declarations.
F0060.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0061.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert existence of a global canonical axis-set Σ* such that Comp(Σ*) and Σ* applies across all compatibility components or admissible domains.
F0061.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMPLETENESS

F0062.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any universal schema asserting that Comp(Σ) implies every admissible axis-like function is generated, derived, or representable by members of Σ.
F0062.label: FORBIDDEN_SPAN_SCHEMA

F0063.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any universal schema asserting uniqueness or equivalence of any two axis-sets satisfying Comp.
F0063.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA

F0064.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema asserting that Orth relations plus Comp imply basis-like structure, orthonormality, or coordinate representation.
F0064.label: FORBIDDEN_ORTHOGONAL_COMPLETENESS_SCHEMA

F0065.body: Admissibility of promoting certain Comp(Σ) claims into basis or frame notions is open, provided these remain removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.
F0065.label: BASIS_EXTENSIONS

F0066.body: Admissibility of spanning-like systems that introduce representability constraints is open, provided no rank, dimensionality, or global coordinate semantics is asserted by default.
F0066.label: SPANNING_SYSTEM_EXTENSIONS

F0067.body: Admissibility of Hilbert-style completeness notions is open, provided they do not bind kernel admissibility and do not assume completed infinities.
F0067.label: HILBERT_COMPLETENESS_EXTENSIONS

F0068.body: This specification presupposes admitted composition symbols for transport composition (when defined), admitted transport instances, and admitted witnessing of order-sensitivity and obstruction under f
F0068.label: DEPENDENCY

F0069.body: A composition-classification is admissible only as an explicitly declared classifier relation ClassComp(CompSym,k) where CompSym ranges over explicitly declared composition symbols and k ranges over a
F0069.label: CLASSCOMP_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0070.body: No admissible composition-classification may assume ClassComp(CompSym,k) is defined for all composition symbols, nor that each CompSym belongs to exactly one class token in K.
F0070.label: CLASSIFICATION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0071.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert or imply any group, ring, field, vector-space, module, or algebraic structure over composition symbols, tokens, or relations.
F0071.label: NONALGEBRA_GUARANTEE

F0072.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert closure of composition within any class or across classes, nor assert that composing two admissible compositions yields an admissible composition sy
F0072.label: NO_CLOSURE_BY_DEFAULT

F0073.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that composition is total on any declared domain of symbols or instances.
F0073.label: NO_TOTALITY_BY_DEFAULT

F0074.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert associativity of composition symbols or composition instances unless explicitly placed in OPEN.
F0074.label: NO_ASSOCIATIVITY_BY_DEFAULT

F0075.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert existence of an identity composition symbol or neutral element unless explicitly placed in OPEN.
F0075.label: NO_IDENTITY_ELEMENT_BY_DEFAULT

F0076.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert invertibility of composition symbols or instances unless explicitly placed in OPEN.
F0076.label: NO_INVERTIBILITY_BY_DEFAULT

F0077.body: Composition classes may be used to distinguish admissible order-sensitivity witnesses, and no admissible schema may force commutation of compositions by default.
F0077.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_CLASS_DISTINCTIONS

F0078.body: No admissible composition-classification may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassComp declarations
F0078.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0079.body: No admissible composition-classification may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassComp declarations.
F0079.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0080.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert a single global partition of all admissible composition symbols into classes by default.
F0080.label: NO_GLOBAL_CLASS_PARTITION

F0081.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that for every composition symbol CompSym there exists k in K with ClassComp(CompSym,k).
F0081.label: FORBIDDEN_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA

F0082.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that for every composition symbol CompSym there exists exactly one k in K with ClassComp(CompSym,k).
F0082.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA

F0083.body: Admissibility of associativity for restricted composition families is open, provided it introduces no algebraic primitives and does not trivialize order-sensitivity witnesses.
F0083.label: ASSOCIATIVITY_EXTENSIONS

F0084.body: Admissibility of identity-like composition symbols for restricted families is open, provided no substitution power or global totality is introduced.
F0084.label: IDENTITY_EXTENSIONS

F0085.body: Admissibility of algebraic structures over composition symbols is open, provided it remains removable and does not bind kernel admissibility.
F0085.label: ALGEBRAIC_EXTENSIONS

F0086.body: This derivation presupposes frozen admissibility of state tokens, transport relations, and obstruction relations.
F0086.label: DEPENDENCY

F0087.body: A minimal global carrier is admissible only as an explicitly declared token registry M whose elements are carrier tokens m.
F0087.label: GLOBAL_CARRIER_SYMBOL

F0088.body: Any admissible global carrier M must admit an explicitly declared indexing relation Ind(s,m) binding each declared state token s to at least one carrier token m in M; no uniqueness is implied.
F0088.label: STATE_INDEXING_MAP

F0089.body: No admissible derivation may assume a canonical distinguished carrier token in M.
F0089.label: NO_CANONICAL_POINTS

F0090.body: Compatibility induced by transport is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation CompM(m,n) over carrier tokens, supported by the existence of at least one admissible transport instance
F0090.label: COMPATIBILITY_RELATION

F0091.body: No admissible derivation may assume CompM(m,n) holds for all carrier token pairs (m,n).
F0091.label: NO_TOTAL_COMPATIBILITY

F0092.body: No admissible derivation may assume that the carrier tokens of M form a single compatibility component under CompM.
F0092.label: NO_CONNECTEDNESS_ASSUMPTION

F0093.body: Obstruction is admissible on the carrier only via an explicitly declared relation ObsM((m,n),(p,q)) whose instances are supported by underlying obstruction instances Obs(τ,κ) together with compatibili
F0093.label: OBSTRUCTION_LIFTING_RELATION

F0094.body: If there exists any admissible obstruction instance Obs(τ,κ), then admissibility requires that the global carrier structure include at least one nontrivial obstruction-lift instance ObsM((m,n),(p,q))
F0094.label: OBSTRUCTION_INDUCED_NONTRIVIALITY

F0095.body: Path organization is admissible only via explicitly declared finite path tokens π and an endpoint relation End(π,m,n) over carrier tokens.
F0095.label: PATH_TOKEN_ADMISSIBILITY

F0096.body: Any asserted End(π,m,n) is admissible only if supported by a finite chain of admissible transport instances Tr(s0,s1),…,Tr(sk-1,sk) together with indexing Ind(si,mi) such that m0=m and mk=n are carrie
F0096.label: PATH_SUPPORT_BY_TRANSPORT

F0097.body: If a concatenation notion is admitted on paths, it is admissible only as an explicitly declared ternary relation Cat(π1,π2,π3) with no associativity, identity, or totality implied.
F0097.label: PATH_CONCATENATION_SYMBOL

F0098.body: Global trivialization of transport is forbidden whenever there exists a witnessed obstruction instance such that two path tokens with the same declared endpoints are supported by transport chains whos
F0098.label: NONFLATTENABILITY_CONDITION

F0099.body: No admissible derivation may include a universal schema asserting that for all carrier tokens m,n there exists a path token π with End(π,m,n) supported by transport and producing indistinguishable com
F0099.label: NO_UNIVERSAL_TRIVIALIZATION_SCHEMA

F0100.body: No admissible derivation may introduce any numeric or ordered distance-like relation on carrier tokens.
F0100.label: FORBIDDEN_METRIC_STRUCTURE

F0101.body: No admissible derivation may introduce any coordinate assignment, chart, or tuple-valued labeling of carrier tokens that carries substitution power.
F0101.label: FORBIDDEN_COORDINATE_STRUCTURE

F0102.body: No admissible derivation may assert any dimensionality property of M.
F0102.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_ASSERTION

F0103.body: Admissibility of introducing geometry-like structure on M is open, provided it is derived from CompM, path organization, and obstruction-lift relations and does not assume metric primitives.
F0103.label: GEOMETRY_AS_EXTENSION

F0104.body: Admissibility of curvature-like invariants defined purely from obstruction-lift behavior and transport composition is open.
F0104.label: CURVATURE_AS_EXTENSION

F0105.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from purely relational properties of M is open, provided no coordinate or metric assumptions are introduced.
F0105.label: DIMENSIONALITY_AS_EXTENSION

F0106.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M, compatibility CompM, path tokens with End and Cat, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, and any admitted metric-like relations Met/MetP.
F0106.label: DEPENDENCY

F0107.body: A coordinate-like representation is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Coord(x,c) where x is a carrier token or path token and c is a token in an explicitly declared finite coordinate-
F0107.label: COORDINATE_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0108.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assume Coord(x,c) exists for all x, nor uniqueness of c for any x.
F0108.label: COORDINATE_MAP_PARTIALITY

F0109.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert that sharing a coordinate token implies identity, equality, or indistinguishability of carrier or path tokens.
F0109.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_COORDINATES

F0110.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may introduce any schema permitting replacement of one token by another on the basis of Coord.
F0110.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_COORDINATES

F0111.body: Any coordinate-like relation Coord is admissible only as a removable representation layer; no admissible specification may treat Coord as constraining or redefining carrier, transport, obstruction, ge
F0111.label: REPRESENTATION_ONLY

F0112.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assume a canonical or privileged coordinate assignment for any token or relation.
F0112.label: NO_CANONICAL_COORDINATIZATION

F0113.body: Any asserted Coord(x,c) is admissible only if supported by at least one frozen relation instance involving x (e.g., CompM, End, Inc, Met), without introducing any new kernel constraint.
F0113.label: COMPATIBILITY_SUPPORT

F0114.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Coord assignments.
F0114.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0115.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Coord assignments.
F0115.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0116.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert the existence of a global coordinate system covering all carrier tokens or all compatible pairs.
F0116.label: NO_GLOBAL_COORDINATE_SYSTEM

F0117.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert any dimensionality, rank, or coordinate-length property of C or of Coord assignments.
F0117.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_SCHEMA

F0118.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may introduce chart or atlas structures with overlap rules unless explicitly declared OPEN in a later extension.
F0118.label: FORBIDDEN_CHART_ATLAS_SCHEMA

F0119.body: Admissibility of chart-like collections of coordinate relations is open, provided no dimensionality, rank, or metric primitives are introduced and Coord remains removable.
F0119.label: CHART_EXTENSIONS

F0120.body: Admissibility of atlas-like structures organizing multiple chart-like collections is open, provided Coord remains non-authoritative and introduces no substitution power.
F0120.label: ATLAS_EXTENSIONS

F0121.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from purely relational properties of coordinate-token registries is open, provided no rank primitives or completed infinities are introduced.
F0121.label: DIMENSIONALITY_EXTENSIONS

F0122.body: Any admissible specification has a finite description over a finite alphabet.
F0122.label: FINITUDE

F0123.body: Any admissible specification declares a finite registry of atomic tokens used as carriers for all subsequent relations.
F0123.label: FINITE_REGISTRY

F0124.body: Admissible composition is not globally commutative; at least one admissible specification contains an order-sensitivity witness as a relation-instance.
F0124.label: NONCOMMUTATION

F0125.body: No admissible specification may designate a privileged token as an identity element for all admissible compositions.
F0125.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_IDENTITY

F0126.body: No admissible specification may introduce a primitive substitutive equality predicate on tokens.
F0126.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_EQUALITY

F0127.body: An admissible specification consists of (i) a finite token registry, (ii) a finite set of relation symbols with declared arities, and (iii) finite relation-instances interpreting those symbols over th
F0127.label: ADMISSIBLE_SPEC_SCHEMA

F0128.body: If a composition notion is admitted, it is represented as a ternary relation Comp(x,y,z) over tokens, with no totality assumed.
F0128.label: COMPOSITION_RELATION

F0129.body: If a distinction notion is admitted, it is represented as a binary relation Dist(x,y) over tokens, with no symmetry assumed.
F0129.label: DISTINGUISHABILITY_RELATION

F0130.body: Order sensitivity is admitted only via finite witnesses of the form Comp(a,b,u), Comp(b,a,v), Dist(u,v), where a,b,u,v are tokens.
F0130.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_WITNESS

F0131.body: No admissible specification may include a universal commutation axiom schema that forces order insensitivity for all composable pairs.
F0131.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMMUTATION_AXIOM

F0132.body: No admissible specification may include a universal substitution schema that permits replacing a token by another token across all relation-instances.
F0132.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_SCHEMA

F0133.body: No admissible specification may include a universal identity-law schema asserting existence of a token e satisfying Comp(e,x,x) and Comp(x,e,x) for all tokens x.
F0133.label: NO_CANONICAL_IDENTITY_LAW

F0134.body: Totality of Comp is not assumed; any closure or total-composability condition must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.
F0134.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0135.body: Associativity of composition is not assumed; any associativity claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.
F0135.label: ASSOCIATIVITY_NOT_ASSUMED

F0136.body: Invertibility of composition is not assumed; any inverse-like claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.
F0136.label: INVERTIBILITY_NOT_ASSUMED

F0137.body: Compatibility of any equivalence-like relation with composition is not assumed; any congruence-like claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.
F0137.label: CONGRUENCE_NOT_ASSUMED

F0138.body: A derived equivalence-like relation may be introduced only as a definition from explicitly declared finite relation-instances; no global substitution power is implied.
F0138.label: DERIVED_EQUIVALENCE

F0139.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared commutative sub-specifications is open, provided no global commutation axiom is imposed on the overall admissible class.
F0139.label: COMMUTATIVE_SUBCLASS

F0140.body: Admissibility of identity-surrogate notions defined purely from declared relations is open, provided no universal identity law or substitution schema is introduced.
F0140.label: IDENTITY_SURROGATE

F0141.body: Criteria for minimal admissible relation vocabularies sufficient to witness noncommutation without primitive equality are open.
F0141.label: MINIMALITY_CRITERIA

F0142.body: This specification presupposes admitted path tokens π formed from admissible transport compositions (when defined), admitted composition symbols, and preserved witnessing of order-sensitivity and obst
F0142.label: DEPENDENCY

F0143.body: A cycle-classification is admissible only as an explicitly declared classifier relation ClassCycle(π,k) where π ranges over admitted path tokens and k ranges over a finite declared cycle-class token r
F0143.label: CLASSCYCLE_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0144.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assume ClassCycle(π,k) is defined for all path tokens, nor that each π belongs to exactly one class token in KC.
F0144.label: CYCLE_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0145.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert or imply temporal recurrence, dynamics, evolution, rates, or causal iteration; cycle is a structural classification of paths only.
F0145.label: NON_TEMPORAL_NON_DYNAMIC_GUARANTEE

F0146.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert or imply engines, work, extraction, advantage, or optimization; cycle is not a machine schema.
F0146.label: NON_ENGINE_GUARANTEE

F0147.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that ClassCycle(π,k) implies identity, equality, equivalence, or indistinguishability of paths, carrier tokens, or state tokens.
F0147.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_CYCLE

F0148.body: No admissible cycle-classification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any path or token on the basis of ClassCycle membership.
F0148.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_CYCLE

F0149.body: No admissible cycle-classification may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassCycle declarations.
F0149.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0150.body: No admissible cycle-classification may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassCycle declarations.
F0150.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0151.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert a mandatory global partition of all admissible paths into cycle classes by default.
F0151.label: NO_GLOBAL_CYCLE_PARTITION

F0152.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that for every admitted path token π there exists k in KC such that ClassCycle(π,k).
F0152.label: FORBIDDEN_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA

F0153.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that for every admitted path token π there exists exactly one k in KC such that ClassCycle(π,k).
F0153.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA

F0154.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert closure of cycle-classes under path concatenation, nor assert that concatenation of two paths in the same class remains in that class, unless explicitly p
F0154.label: FORBIDDEN_CLOSURE_SCHEMA

F0155.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that each class k has a canonical representative path πk or canonical normal form by default.
F0155.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_REPRESENTATIVE_SCHEMA

F0156.body: Admissibility of recurrence-like interpretations is open, provided they do not introduce time, probability, causality, or optimization as primitives.
F0156.label: RECURRENCE_EXTENSIONS

F0157.body: Admissibility of dynamics-like structure on paths is open, provided it remains removable and does not bind kernel admissibility.
F0157.label: DYNAMICS_EXTENSIONS

F0158.body: Admissibility of engine-like interpretations of cycle classes is open, provided they remain non-binding overlays and introduce no optimization or extraction semantics into the kernel.
F0158.label: ENGINE_EXTENSIONS

F0159.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M with compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, geometry relations, and optional removable coordinate relations Coord.
F0159.label: DEPENDENCY

F0160.body: A dimensionality-like claim is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Dim(A,d) where A is a token denoting a finite declared family of relation instances or representation instances, and d
F0160.label: DIMENSION_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0161.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may treat dimension tokens d as numbers, ordered quantities, or elements of a completed infinite domain.
F0161.label: DIMENSION_TOKEN_NONNUMERIC

F0162.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce rank primitives, basis primitives, or coordinate-length primitives as part of Dim.
F0162.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_RANK

F0163.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assume that Dim(A,d) is defined for all admissible families A, nor that any canonical d exists for any A.
F0163.label: NO_GLOBAL_DIMENSIONALITY

F0164.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert that equality of dimension tokens implies identity, equality, or substitution among carrier tokens, paths, or relations.
F0164.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_DIM

F0165.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or relation instance on the basis of Dim.
F0165.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_DIM

F0166.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may include any schema forcing transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Dim declarations.
F0166.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0167.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may include any schema forcing obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Dim declarations.
F0167.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0168.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assume dimensionality claims exist for all compatibility components of M.
F0168.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0169.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce any axis system, axis basis, coordinate rank, or vector-space basis structure at this layer.
F0169.label: FORBIDDEN_AXIS_SYSTEM

F0170.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert that any family A has a unique canonical dimension token d independent of admissible representation choices.
F0170.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_DIMENSION

F0171.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert any universal additivity or product-like rule for Dim over composition of families A.
F0171.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_ADDITIVITY_SCHEMA

F0172.body: Admissibility of axis-system notions derived from Dim is open, provided they are removable representations and introduce no substitution power.
F0172.label: AXIS_EXTENSIONS

F0173.body: Admissibility of coordinate-rank notions is open, provided rank is not treated as primitive and dimensionality remains nonnumeric and non-authoritative.
F0173.label: COORDINATE_RANK_EXTENSIONS

F0174.body: Admissibility of vector-space-like structures is open, provided they are introduced without assuming completed infinities and without binding kernel admissibility.
F0174.label: VECTOR_SPACE_EXTENSIONS

F0175.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier/state token admission, transport and obstruction instances, and optional axis-function and axis-set structures, without assuming any total classification
F0175.label: DEPENDENCY

F0176.body: A duality-like structure is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Dual(x,y) or an explicitly declared classifier relation Class2(x,c) where x ranges over admitted carrier to
F0176.label: DUALITY_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0177.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assume Dual(x,y) or Class2(x,c) is defined for all admissible x, nor that every x belongs to exactly one class token in C2.
F0177.label: DUALITY_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0178.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert or imply two kinds of being, substance, or necessity; duality is classification of admitted tokens only.
F0178.label: NONONTOLOGY_GUARANTEE

F0179.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert or imply axes, coordinate frames, bases, rank, dimensionality, or any global parameterization of the carrier.
F0179.label: NONAXIS_NONCOORDINATE_GUARANTEE

F0180.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that duality or class membership implies identity, equality, or indistinguishability of tokens or relation instances.
F0180.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_DUALITY

F0181.body: No admissible duality-like structure may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or relation instance on the basis of Dual or Class2 membership.
F0181.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_DUALITY

F0182.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to duality declarations.
F0182.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0183.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to duality declarations.
F0183.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0184.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any universal schema asserting a mandatory two-class partition of all admissible objects in any declared domain.
F0184.label: NO_GLOBAL_PARTITION_SCHEMA

F0185.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that for every x there exists c in C2 such that Class2(x,c).
F0185.label: FORBIDDEN_CLASS_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA

F0186.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that for every x there exists exactly one c in C2 such that Class2(x,c).
F0186.label: FORBIDDEN_CLASS_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA

F0187.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert existence of a universal involution map Inv(x) or equivalence-like pairing schema derived from Dual by default.
F0187.label: FORBIDDEN_INVOLUTION_SCHEMA

F0188.body: Admissibility of involution-like maps is open, provided they introduce no identity, equality, substitution power, or global totality by default.
F0188.label: INVOLUTION_EXTENSIONS

F0189.body: Admissibility of conjugation-like pairings is open, provided they remain structural classifications and do not induce coordinate or metric structure.
F0189.label: CONJUGATION_EXTENSIONS

F0190.body: Admissibility of symmetry-like notions relating duality classes is open, provided no ontological necessity or global partition is asserted.
F0190.label: SYMMETRY_EXTENSIONS

F0191.body: This specification presupposes all frozen admissibility layers for carrier/state/transport/obstruction, composition-classes, and cycle-classes, and presupposes that no time-like, probability-like, cau
F0191.label: DEPENDENCY

F0192.body: No dynamics-like notion (including evolution, flow, rate, recurrence, update, or temporal iteration) is admissible as kernel structure under the frozen layers without admitting additional primitives b
F0192.label: BOUNDARY_STATEMENT

F0193.body: A time-like primitive capable of supporting ordered progression is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.
F0193.label: TIME_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT

F0194.body: A probability-like primitive capable of supporting stochastic update semantics is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.
F0194.label: PROBABILITY_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT

F0195.body: A causality-like primitive capable of supporting directed dependence claims is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.
F0195.label: CAUSALITY_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT

F0196.body: Any kernel claim asserting existence of a global update rule, step-indexed evolution, time-parameterized family of state maps, rate law, recurrence law, or convergent iteration is forbidden under the
F0196.label: FORBIDDEN_DYNAMICS_CLAIMS

F0197.body: Any kernel claim asserting “before/after,” “next/previous,” temporal ordering of states, or temporal interpretation of paths is forbidden under the frozen layers.
F0197.label: FORBIDDEN_TEMPORAL_SEMANTICS

F0198.body: Any kernel claim asserting stochastic transitions, sampling, random evolution, or probabilistic trajectories is forbidden under the frozen layers.
F0198.label: FORBIDDEN_STOCHASTIC_SEMANTICS

F0199.body: Any kernel claim asserting causal forcing, directed causal arrows, or causal explanation for refinement, transport, obstruction, composition-class, or cycle-class behavior is forbidden under the froze
F0199.label: FORBIDDEN_CAUSAL_SEMANTICS

F0200.body: Any future admission of dynamics-like primitives or dynamics-like structures must not retroactively alter admissibility of any earlier layer, and must not reclassify previously forbidden structures as
F0200.label: NON_BACKFLOW_GUARANTEE

F0201.body: Dynamics-like notions may appear only as removable, non-binding overlays that do not constrain or modify kernel admissibility, and any such overlay must remain deletable without changing any kernel-de
F0201.label: SAFE_OVERLAY_BOUNDARY

F0202.body: A future DYNAMICS_ADMISSIBILITY_v1 is open only if it explicitly admits additional primitives, explicitly scopes which dynamics schemas become admissible, and explicitly preserves non-identity, non-su
F0202.label: FUTURE_DYNAMICS_ADMISSIBILITY_CONDITIONS

F0203.body: This specification presupposes a frozen global carrier M with compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, and obstruction Obs/ObsM as previously admitted.
F0203.label: DEPENDENCY

F0204.body: A geometric structure on M is admissible only as a finite collection of explicitly declared relation symbols over carrier tokens and path tokens, together with finite relation-instances, and with no a
F0204.label: GEOMETRIC_STRUCTURE_SCHEMA

F0205.body: An incidence notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Inc(m,π,n) supported by End(π,m,n) and compatibility CompM(m,n).
F0205.label: INCIDENCE_RELATION_ADMISSIBILITY

F0206.body: A locality notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation Loc(m,n) whose instances are supported by CompM(m,n); no symmetry, reflexivity, or transitivity is implied.
F0206.label: LOCALITY_RELATION_ADMISSIBILITY

F0207.body: No admissible geometric structure may introduce any numeric-valued or ordered distance-like, norm-like, or length-like primitive on carrier tokens or paths.
F0207.label: NO_METRIC_PRIMITIVES

F0208.body: No admissible geometric structure may include a universal schema deriving a distance-like ordering from compatibility, transport, or obstruction.
F0208.label: NO_METRIC_DERIVATION_SCHEMA

F0209.body: No admissible geometric structure may introduce any coordinate assignment, chart, tuple-labeling, or coordinate-valued function on carrier tokens.
F0209.label: NO_COORDINATE_ASSIGNMENT

F0210.body: No admissible geometric structure may assert any dimensionality property of M or any coordinate-rank property.
F0210.label: NO_DIMENSION_ASSERTION

F0211.body: Any admissible geometric relation that references paths must be supported by declared transport chains witnessing End(π,m,n); no independent path semantics is admissible.
F0211.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBLE_GEOMETRY

F0212.body: Any admissible geometric relation that references concatenation must be supported by explicitly declared Cat(π1,π2,π3) instances and must not assume associativity or identity of concatenation.
F0212.label: TRANSPORT_COMPOSITION_COMPATIBILITY

F0213.body: No admissible geometric structure may include any schema forcing all transport-supported path composites between the same endpoints to be indistinguishable whenever a witnessed obstruction instance ex
F0213.label: OBSTRUCTION_RESPECT

F0214.body: No admissible geometric structure may include a universal schema asserting global flattenability of transport-supported path composites across all compatible endpoints.
F0214.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_FLAT_GEOMETRY_SCHEMA

F0215.body: No admissible geometric structure may assert existence of an embedding of M into any coordinate-bearing or metric-bearing ambient structure.
F0215.label: FORBIDDEN_EMBEDDING_ASSERTION

F0216.body: No admissible geometric structure may assume continuity, limits, convergence, or openness properties of any relation on M.
F0216.label: FORBIDDEN_CONTINUITY_ASSUMPTION

F0217.body: Admissibility of metric-like structures is open, provided they are derived without introducing metric primitives and do not trivialize obstruction.
F0217.label: METRIC_EXTENSIONS

F0218.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions is open, provided no coordinate or rank primitives are introduced.
F0218.label: DIMENSION_EXTENSIONS

F0219.body: Admissibility of coordinate-like representations is open, provided they are treated as removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.
F0219.label: COORDINATE_EXTENSIONS

F0220.body: This specification presupposes a frozen carrier M, compatibility CompM, path tokens π with End, transport Tr/TrC, and obstruction Obs/ObsM, and a frozen geometry layer.
F0220.label: DEPENDENCY

F0221.body: A metric-like structure is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Met(m,n,v) or MetP(π,σ,v) where v is a token from an explicitly declared finite value-token registry V.
F0221.label: METRIC_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0222.body: No admissible metric-like structure may treat value tokens v as numbers, ordered quantities, or elements of a completed infinite domain.
F0222.label: VALUE_TOKEN_NONNUMERIC

F0223.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assume Met(m,n,·) is defined for all carrier token pairs (m,n), nor that for any pair there exists any v.
F0223.label: NO_GLOBAL_METRIC_TOTALITY

F0224.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assume a canonical distinguished value token (e.g., a “zero” or “unit” value) across all Met instances.
F0224.label: NO_CANONICAL_VALUE

F0225.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema that forces all transport-supported path composites between the same endpoints to be indistinguishable whenever a witnessed obstruction exist
F0225.label: NONTRIVIALITY_PRESERVATION

F0226.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema that forces Met-consistency to imply commutation of transport composition in cases where order-sensitivity has been witnessed.
F0226.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0227.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assert that Met(m,n,v) implies identity or equality of m and n, nor introduce substitution power from Met.
F0227.label: NO_EQUALITY_FROM_METRIC

F0228.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting a triangle-like law over value tokens.
F0228.label: FORBIDDEN_TRIANGLE_SCHEMA

F0229.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting Met(m,n,v) implies Met(n,m,v) for all m,n,v.
F0229.label: FORBIDDEN_SYMMETRY_SCHEMA

F0230.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting existence of v such that Met(m,m,v) for all m.
F0230.label: FORBIDDEN_REFLEXIVITY_SCHEMA

F0231.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema asserting that distinctness is implied by non-canonical values, or that canonical values imply indistinguishability.
F0231.label: FORBIDDEN_SEPARATION_SCHEMA

F0232.body: Any asserted Met(m,n,v) is admissible only if supported by CompM(m,n) and the existence of at least one path token π with End(π,m,n) supported by transport chains.
F0232.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBLE_METRIC_SUPPORT

F0233.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assert Met instances on token pairs lacking any geometry-layer support relation that connects those tokens via compatibility or incidence.
F0233.label: GEOMETRY_COMPATIBLE_METRIC

F0234.body: Admissibility of introducing an order relation on value tokens is open, provided it does not introduce completed infinities, numeric primitives, or optimization semantics.
F0234.label: ORDERED_VALUE_EXTENSIONS

F0235.body: Admissibility of coordinate-like representations derived from Met is open, provided they are removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.
F0235.label: COORDINATE_EXTENSIONS

F0236.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from Met is open, provided no coordinate or rank primitives are introduced.
F0236.label: DIMENSION_EXTENSIONS

F0237.body: Admissibility of norm-like structures is open, provided they are admitted as relations over finite value-token registries without numeric primitives.
F0237.label: NORMED_SPACE_EXTENSIONS

F0238.body: Any admissible obstruction specification is given by a finite description over a finite alphabet.
F0238.label: FINITUDE

F0239.body: Any admissible obstruction specification presupposes frozen state abstraction and transport layers and ranges only over declared transport-related relation instances.
F0239.label: DEPENDENCY

F0240.body: An obstruction notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Obs(τ,κ), where τ and κ are transport-related relation instances over state tokens.
F0240.label: OBSTRUCTION_SYMBOL_ADMISSIBILITY

F0241.body: Any asserted Obs(τ,κ) is admissible only if τ and κ are explicitly declared transport-related relation instances formed from symbols admitted in the transport layer.
F0241.label: OBSTRUCTION_WELLTYPEDNESS

F0242.body: A failure of transport trivialization is admissible only as a finite witnessed claim that two transport-related composites are distinguishable by the frozen state distinguishability relation.
F0242.label: NONTRIVIALIZATION_FAILURE_FORM

F0243.body: If transport composition is used in an obstruction claim, it must be represented only via explicitly declared instances of TrC(·,·,·) over state tokens; no implicit composite formation is admissible.
F0243.label: COMPOSITE_TRANSPORT_INSTANCE

F0244.body: Any admissible obstruction witness must have the finite pattern TrC(a,b,u), TrC(c,d,v), DistS(u,v), together with admissible transport-witness bindings supporting the involved transport assertions; no
F0244.label: FAILURE_WITNESS_PATTERN

F0245.body: No admissible obstruction specification may introduce any geometric, metric, coordinate, neighborhood, or embedding interpretation for Obs or its witnesses.
F0245.label: NO_GEOMETRIC_READING

F0246.body: Any assertion of Obs(τ,κ) is admissible only if accompanied by a finite witness binding WObs(τ,κ,w), where w is a token serving solely as a syntactic witness carrier.
F0246.label: OBSTRUCTION_WITNESSING_BINDING

F0247.body: No admissible obstruction specification may treat witness tokens as semantic evidence, valuation, score, or truth-bearer; WObs binds only syntactic admissibility support.
F0247.label: WITNESS_NONSEMANTIC

F0248.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assert that Obs(τ,κ) implies identity or equality of any involved state tokens or transport instances.
F0248.label: NONIDENTITY_FROM_OBSTRUCTION

F0249.body: No admissible obstruction specification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any state token or transport instance on the basis of Obs(τ,κ).
F0249.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_OBSTRUCTION

F0250.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) holds for all transport-related instance pairs.
F0250.label: OBSTRUCTION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0251.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,τ) holds for all transport-related instances τ.
F0251.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONREFLEXIVE_DEFAULT

F0252.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) implies Obs(κ,τ).
F0252.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONSYMMETRIC_DEFAULT

F0253.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) and Obs(κ,λ) imply Obs(τ,λ).
F0253.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONTRANSITIVE_DEFAULT

F0254.body: No admissible obstruction specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing absence of obstruction for all transport-related instance pairs.
F0254.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_FLATNESS_AXIOM

F0255.body: No admissible obstruction specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing obstruction for all transport-related instance pairs.
F0255.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_OBSTRUCTION_AXIOM

F0256.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assert uniqueness or canonicity of obstruction witnesses beyond explicitly declared finite witnesses.
F0256.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_OBSTRUCTION

F0257.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared higher-arity relations aggregating multiple obstruction instances is open, provided no geometric, metric, temporal, or motion semantics are introduced.
F0257.label: CURVATURE_LIKE_RELATIONS

F0258.body: Admissibility of class tokens representing families of obstruction-witness patterns is open, provided no primitive equality or substitution schema is introduced.
F0258.label: HOLONOMY_CLASS_TOKENS

F0259.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared local trivialization schemes (restricted collections of transport instances where obstruction is absent) is open, provided no geometry, neighborhood, or metric lan
F0259.label: TRIVIALIZATION_SCHEMES

F0260.body: This specification presupposes admitted axis-like function symbols F,G,… and admitted finite axis-sets Σ with membership Mem(Σ,F), under frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.
F0260.label: DEPENDENCY

F0261.body: An orthogonality-like relation is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Orth(F,G) ranging over admitted axis-like function relation symbols.
F0261.label: ORTHOGONALITY_SYMBOL_SCHEMA

F0262.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assume Orth(F,G) is defined for all pairs of function symbols, nor assume uniqueness or completeness of Orth structure.
F0262.label: ORTH_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0263.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert or imply any metric, norm, distance, or inner product structure on carrier tokens, state tokens, path tokens, or value-token registries.
F0263.label: NONMETRIC_GUARANTEE

F0264.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert or imply bases, coordinate frames, rank, completeness, dimensionality, or spanning properties for any axis-set or family of functions.
F0264.label: NOBASIS_NORANK_GUARANTEE

F0265.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert that Orth(F,G) implies identity, equality, or substitutability of function symbols or any tokens they reference.
F0265.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_ORTH

F0266.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may introduce any schema permitting replacement of tokens or function symbols on the basis of Orth.
F0266.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_ORTH

F0267.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Orth declarations.
F0267.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION

F0268.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Orth declarations.
F0268.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION

F0269.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,G) implies Orth(G,F) for all F,G.
F0269.label: FORBIDDEN_SYMMETRY_SCHEMA

F0270.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,F) for all F.
F0270.label: FORBIDDEN_REFLEXIVITY_SCHEMA

F0271.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,G) and Orth(G,H) implies Orth(F,H).
F0271.label: FORBIDDEN_TRANSITIVITY_SCHEMA

F0272.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert existence of a global orthogonal family or global orthogonalization procedure over all admitted functions.
F0272.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_ORTHOGONAL_SET

F0273.body: Admissibility of inner-product-like structures is open, provided they are introduced without assuming metrics, bases, rank, or dimensionality as primitives.
F0273.label: INNER_PRODUCT_EXTENSIONS

F0274.body: Admissibility of normed-space-like structure is open, provided it does not introduce completed infinities and does not bind kernel admissibility.
F0274.label: NORMED_SPACE_EXTENSIONS

F0275.body: Admissibility of Hilbert-like structures is open, provided they remain removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.
F0275.label: HILBERT_LIKE_EXTENSIONS

F0276.body: Any admissible transport specification is given by a finite description over a finite alphabet.
F0276.label: FINITUDE

F0277.body: Any admissible transport specification presupposes a frozen state abstraction layer and ranges only over declared state tokens.
F0277.label: DEPENDENCY

F0278.body: A transport notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Tr(s,t) over the finite state-token registry.
F0278.label: TRANSPORT_SYMBOL_ADMISSIBILITY

F0279.body: Any asserted Tr(s,t) is admissible only if both s and t are declared state tokens in the state-token registry.
F0279.label: TRANSPORT_WELLTYPEDNESS

F0280.body: No admissible transport specification may assert that Tr(s,t) implies identity or equality of s and t.
F0280.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_TRANSPORT

F0281.body: No admissible transport specification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of s by t (or t by s) in arbitrary relations on the basis of Tr(s,t).
F0281.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_TRANSPORT

F0282.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) holds for all ordered pairs of state tokens.
F0282.label: TRANSPORT_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT

F0283.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,s) holds for all state tokens s.
F0283.label: TRANSPORT_NONREFLEXIVE_DEFAULT

F0284.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) implies Tr(t,s).
F0284.label: TRANSPORT_NONSYMMETRIC_DEFAULT

F0285.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) and Tr(t,u) imply Tr(s,u).
F0285.label: TRANSPORT_NONTRANSITIVE_DEFAULT

F0286.body: If a composition of transport is admitted, it is admissible only as an explicitly declared ternary relation symbol TrC(s,t,u) with no closure, associativity, commutativity, identity, or invertibility
F0286.label: TRANSPORT_COMPOSITION_SYMBOL

F0287.body: Any claim that transport composition is order-sensitive is admissible only via finite witnesses of the form TrC(a,b,u), TrC(b,a,v), DistS(u,v), where DistS is an admissible distinguishability relation
F0287.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_WITNESS

F0288.body: No admissible transport specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing TrC(a,b,·) to be indistinguishable from TrC(b,a,·) for all a,b.
F0288.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMMUTATION_OF_TRANSPORT

F0289.body: Any assertion of Tr(s,t) is admissible only if accompanied by a finite witness binding WTr(s,t,w), where w is a token serving solely as a syntactic witness carrier.
F0289.label: TRANSPORT_WITNESSING_BINDING

F0290.body: No admissible transport specification may treat witness tokens as semantic evidence, valuation, score, or truth-bearers; WTr binds only syntactic admissibility support.
F0290.label: WITNESS_NONSEMANTIC

F0291.body: Any admissible WTr(s,t,w) must be supported by finite declared bindings Abs(s,Σs), Abs(t,Σt) and declared representatives Rep(s,x), Rep(t,y) such that a declared relation instance R(x,y) exists in at
F0291.label: WITNESS_SUPPORT_REQUIREMENT

F0292.body: No admissible transport specification may assert that transport trivializes globally by forcing Tr(s,t) for all state-token pairs.
F0292.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_TRIVIALIZATION

F0293.body: No admissible transport specification may assert the existence of a universal transport identity law making Tr(s,s) hold for all s.
F0293.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_IDENTITY_TRANSPORT

F0294.body: No admissible transport specification may assert uniqueness or canonicity of transport between any ordered pair (s,t) without explicit finite witness declarations.
F0294.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_TRANSPORT

F0295.body: Admissibility of an explicitly declared inverse-like relation InvTr(t,s) associated to Tr(s,t) is open, provided no substitution schema or identity/equality is introduced.
F0295.label: INVERTIBLE_TRANSPORT

F0296.body: Admissibility of derived equivalence-like quotients induced by transport relations is open, provided no primitive equality or universal substitution is introduced.
F0296.label: TRANSPORT_EQUIVALENCE_CLASSES

F0297.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared loop-composition effects (nontrivial composites of transport around declared cycles) is open, provided no geometric, metric, temporal, or motion semantics are intr
F0297.label: TRANSPORT_LOOP_EFFECTS
